observations.recommendation:
3. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Appraisal Committee to submit a report of its assessment on the value of the donated vehicles to the Municipal Accountant.
4. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the donated vehicles in the books of the Municipality using the assessment of the Appraisal Committee and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Combined Financial Statements as required in PPSAS 1 and 17.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management remain to date. Please refer to related finding in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Rescue vehicles unrecorded in the books of accounts. Please refer to related finding in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommended that the LGU appropriate and utilize its 20% Development Fund strictly within the guidelines provided in Sections 3 and 2 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and DILG,DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01 dated March 27, 2020, respectively.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management to date.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommended that the BAC complete the posting at the PhilGEPS and GPPB website of the Purchase Order, Contracts and other pertinent documents in compliance with the on-line posting under the requirements in Republic Act No. 9184 and other relevant issuances.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. Still being complied.
observations.reason: Emergency procurement activities were undertaken by the Municipality for CY 2021. However, a compliance with online posting was not undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
11. The Accounting Office to draw a journal entry voucher (JEV) to set-up an allowance for depreciation for the Road Network; Parks, Plaza and Monuments; and the Disaster Risk Reduction Equipment accounts; and
12. The officers concerned strictly observe the accounting and reporting requirements for the local road network construction and maintenance system already prepared by Municipal Engineering but still needs to be updated. 2. Disclosure on Notes to FS 3. Report on Local Road Network by GSO 4. LRN Property Cards
observations.managementAction: No action implemented yet. The SL should be updated but still needs to be updated. Disclosure on Notes to FS not complied yet.
observations.reason: Please see related finding in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: 13. In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, issue Demand letters to debtors to enforce collections or institute/file appropriate charges to defaulting debtors, for unjustifiable failure to settle the account; and 14. Draw JEV to set-up an allowance for impairment of Due from LGU and of Other Receivables.
observations.managementAction: No action. As of March 2021, six barangays have submitted their liquidation. Draw JEV to set-up an allowance for impairment not yet done.
observations.reason: An allowance has not been set up yet.

observations.recommendation: 15. We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant be required to analyze the reciprocal accounts to determine the causes of the discrepancies and adjust the books accordingly; henceforth, to periodically reconcile the reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management to date.
observations.reason: Unreconciled differences between these two accounts still exist. Please refer to related finding in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: 27. Municipal Accountant-Designate record depreciation for CY 2016 and keep property ledger cards and account for the difference between the increase in PPE during the year and the recorded acquisitions in the amount of P3,612,661.17.
observations.managementAction: No action implemented for selected PPE accounts.
observations.reason: Please refer to related finding in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: 30. the Municipal Accountant account for the difference of P4,575,050.00 in the recorded checks issued.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant-Designate is still in the process of reconciling the difference.

observations.recommendation: 32. a justification why the structures for the three (3) subprojects were not fit for occupancy; and 33. proof of approval/review by DILG of the POWs of the three subprojects.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. The Team were not furnished with the copy of the Memo to the Municipal Engineer. Also the POWs were not submitted.
observations.reason: Justification and proof of approval/review by DILG of the POWs were not submitted to the Audit Team as of the report date.

observations.recommendation: 34. an explanation why the POWs for the three subprojects was not submitted when it was a condition for the release of the funds; and 35. proof that the revised POWs were submitted, if any.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. The Memo to the Municipal Engineer were not submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Justification and proof of submission of the POWs were not provided to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: 36. We recommend that the Municipal Engineer be required to submit a justification for the variation in the above-cited subprojects and justification for the approved/reviewed Program of Works for the variation.
observations.managementAction: No action. The Memo to the Municipal Engineer were not submitted to the Audit Team as of the report date.
observations.reason: Justification for the variation and proof of approval/review by the DILG of the subprojects POWs were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: 40. Municipal Accountant to record the collections for the sale of bid documents amounting to P30,000.00; and 41. trace the cause of the negative balance of P260.00 which was carried over from prior years and adjust for the correct entry thereon; 42. Municipal Treasurer to submit the complete transaction documents for proper recording to avoid similar incidents.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. Difficulty in retrieving data.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving data. The required documents not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: 44. submit justification / explanation why the persons responsible will not be held liable for the loss of the Disbursement Vouchers and Liquidation Reports for evaluation by the Auditor, without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Suspension / Disallowance, if any.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: 46. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Accountant to submit to the Auditor all transactions which are already consummated pursuant to Sec. 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should be directed to submit the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) together with disbursement vouchers to the Municipal Accountant pursuant to Sec. 43 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommend that the LGU expedite action in complying with the 20% Development Fund recommendations.
observations.managementAction: Minimal utilization. There was minimal compliance in utilizing the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: There was minimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: 19. The Sanggunian formulate and pass a local GAD ordinance with the assistance of the GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans; and 20. The GFPS of the Municipality regularly monitor the implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that GAD PPAs are effectively and efficiently implemented to attain GAD objective.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. However, some GAD activities were implemented.
observations.reason: GAD Focal Point System was created but some GAD activities were not implemented due to Covid-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: 21. We recommended that Management submit at the beginning of the year a list of on-going and to be implemented projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for the current year, and to inform COA within ten (10) days after the infra-award of the projects regarding the posting of the project signboard or public notice.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. The required project signboard posting information was not submitted.
observations.reason: The required listing and proof of posting were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: 24. Strengthen GAD Focal Point System and establish GAD Database; and 20. The GFPS regularly monitor implementation of GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. However, GAD Focal Point System created and some GAD activities implemented.
observations.reason: Most of GAD activities were not implemented due to Covid-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: 25. We recommend that the GSO-Designate be required to update the Property Cards and the Municipal Accountant to maintain all the Property Ledger Cards, and reconcile their records with the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction: No action implemented yet. GSO/Property Office/Supply Officer-Designate were required to update their records and conduct reconciliations.
observations.reason: GSO/Property Office records not updated; reconciliation not conducted.

observations.recommendation: 27. record depreciation and maintain property ledger cards.
observations.managementAction: No action implemented for selected PPE accounts.
observations.reason: See Part II related findings.

observations.recommendation: 30. Municipal Accountant to reconcile and account for the difference.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Reconciliation in process.

observations.recommendation: 32. submit justification why structures not fit and 33. submit proof of DILG approval/review of POWs.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. Proofs not furnished.
observations.reason: Proofs and justifications not submitted.

observations.recommendation: 34. explain why POWs not submitted; 35. provide proof that revised POWs were submitted if any.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. Memos not submitted to Audit Team.
observations.reason: POWs not submitted; Memo to Municipal Engineer not provided.

observations.recommendation: 36. Municipal Engineer to submit justification for variation and approved/reviewed POWs.
observations.managementAction: No action. Memo to Municipal Engineer not submitted.
observations.reason: Justification and POW approvals not provided.

observations.recommendation: 40. record collections for sale of bid documents; 41. trace negative balance of P260.00; 42. submit complete transaction documents.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. Difficulty in retrieving data.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving data; documents not available.

observations.recommendation: 44. submit justification why persons responsible will not be held liable for loss of DVs and liquidation reports.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: 46. LCE to direct Accountant to submit consummated transactions to Auditor; Treasurer to submit RCI with DVs to Accountant.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create a new Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of
members that will be committed to conduct a complete annual physical
count of LGU properties;
b. issue a directive on the newly created Inventory Committee to:
i. prepare, for her approval, a Physical Inventory Plan containing the
assignments of Committee members, schedules/location of inventory-
taking and cut-off dates for the complete physical inventory of all
properties of the Municipality over a three-month period in CY 2022;
ii. implement the PIP and finish the physical inventory within the time
frame specified in CY 2022; and
iii. submit the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant-Designate before
the end of CY 2022 for reconciliation with book balances.
c. require the GSO and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain
complete Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards respectively, and to
reconcile their records annually.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. coordinate with the donor-agencies (PCSO, the Office of Senator Cynthia
Villar, and the Provincial Government of Leyte) to secure the Deed of
Donation or the bill of lading/airway bill/parcel notice and other related
documents which contains the declared value of the donated PPEs, as basis
in recording the assets in the books of accounts of the LGU;
b. require the GSO, in case no document is provided by the donor-agencies, to
conduct a research, either in the internet or through market survey, to
obtain the fair market value of the donated assets;
c. require the GSO to furnish to the Municipal Accountant-Designate all the
supporting documents relative to the donated PPEs and thereafter require
the latter to record in the LGU books all properties acquired through
donation and adjust the carrying amount of the said properties.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-
Designate and/or GSO Officer to:
a) reconcile the CIP balances at year-end with the records of the Municipal
Engineering Office and secure the documents relative to all on-going
construction projects to ensure the accuracy of the CIP balances that will
be reflected in the Statement of Financial Position;
b) prepare an adjusting entry on the ₱2,184,700.62 misclassified accounts; and
c) resolve the ₱897,432.03 unsupported CIP – Infrastructure assets, in order
that the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the affected
accounts may be made.

4.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate prepare a
Lapsing Schedule of Depreciation for all depreciable PPEs, compute the
accumulated depreciation for the depreciable assets totaling ₱56,150,146.28 for
which no depreciation was recognized to date, while making the necessary
entries to correct the over-depreciation on depreciable assets amounting to
₱3,512,105.76.

5.5 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal
Accountant - Designate with the necessary data regarding the Road Networks
of the Municipality of Pastrana as basis of the latter in providing the complete
disclosure on the details of the Road Networks account in the Notes to the
Financial Statements.

6.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant –
Designate to:
a. examine the causes of the unreconciled difference between the balance per
books and balance per bank of ₱13,137,242.10 and effect the necessary
adjustments;
b. prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly BRS for all bank
accounts within the prescribed period to prove the correctness of the bank
accounts per books and per bank;
c. prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for all bank accounts to facilitate
easy verification of individual balances;
d. investigate the details and supporting documents for the unverified staled
checks of ₱490,791.14 and ₱13,240.54 under the IRA and Calamity Fund
and thereafter draw a Journal Entry Voucher for the restoration of cash and
the corresponding liability account, where necessary; and
e. inquire with the LBP on the status of the DECS TEEP Account No. 0182-
1078-40, and thereafter conduct an account analysis whether the account
balance is due for return to the National Treasury.

7.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to inquire from
the LandBank of the Philippines about the updated status of their PPSB
Savings Account and the requirements for closing the balance of said bank
account;
b. Municipal Accountant-Designate to perform reconciliation of bank balance
with the balance per books prior to closure, to ensure correctness of
amounts and avoid recurrence of unadjusted balances; and
c. Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit to the Audit Team for future
reference, a copy of the documents to support the closure of the PPSB
Savings Account, including the analysis of the CIB-LCSA account and
information on the disposition of the balances.
7.8 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang
Bayan to draft a resolution authorizing the closure of said bank account, should
it be among the required documents to the disposition of the PPSB Savings
Account.

8.6 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor:
a) Compile the data on real properties and prepare a certified list showing
the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible based on the
RPTAR; and
b) Submit to the Municipal Accountant-Designate at the start of the year,
for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to record the
Deferred RPT and SET Receivables of the Municipality.
8.7 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate resolve
and review the discrepancy of ₱35,703.21 between the RPT and SET
Receivable accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting entries, where
necessary.

9.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate reconcile the
reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and prepare
the necessary adjusting entry to ensure that the adjustment made in the
receivable account has an equivalent corresponding adjustment in the payable
account.

10.11 We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer:
a) Obtain from Leyte Provincial Health Office the actual information on
the unit cost and quantities of the vaccines received by the LGU in
relation to its vaccination program;
b) Provide the Municipal Accountant-Designate with the complete
documents from the DOH/LPHO relative to the receipt of the vaccines,
including the extracted data from the Vaccine Operations Reporting
System on the doses administered at the end of each month.
10.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate record using
the perpetual inventory system, the vaccines received by the LGU and the doses
dispensed, using as reference the report/documents submitted by the MHO.

11.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the BAC Secretariat to
consolidate the items in the PPMPs into a single indicative Annual
Procurement Plan based on each group’s unique profile or description to avoid
piecemeal procurement and as a basis in deciding the appropriate mode of
procurement.

12.7 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Project Management
Team:
a) conduct constant and rigid monitoring of contract completion dates
of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality;
b) apply promptly, when necessary, the calibrated actions prescribed
under GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 dated March 8, 2019 on
infrastructure projects with perceived negative slippages; and
c) impose and collect liquidated damages for the unperformed portion
of the works for every day of delay.

13.16 We recommended that in the ensuing years, the Municipal Development
Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, in
coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, identify and rank projects
that only effectively meet the requirements of urgency and those that can be
implemented within the specific time schedules during the budget year in order
to strengthen the plan-budget process linkage of the Municipality.
13.17 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor resolve the root causes of
unimplemented projects, ensure that selected PPAs are substantially compliant
with screening requirements and provide adequate technical personnel to its
Project Management Team.

1. We recommended that the GSO, the MDRRM Officer procured out and the representative from the Office of the Mayor, who were required to conduct an inventory of the relief goods, prepare the corresponding report and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording so that the correct amount of Inventory account will be reflected in the books; and
2. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the balance of the inventory held for distribution after reconciliation with the inventory report of the GSO.

5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the 29 Punong Barangays in the Municipality to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant, the Liquidation Report on the financial assistance granted to them in the amount of P266,000.00 each, as required in the Memorandum of Agreement, for validation as to the propriety of the disbursements made.
observations.managementAction: Follow up conducted on the submission
observations.reason: Upon inquiry with the Municipal Accountant-Designate, 15 barangays to date have submitted their liquidation documents. As of March 2021, six barangays already submitted the documents.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prepare a supplemental budget when changes are made in the projects. 7. We further recommended that the Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant process DVs for payment of expenses only when there is an approved appropriation pursuant to Section 4(1) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 9. We recommended that the MDRRMO utilize the MDRRMF Capital Outlay appropriations by implementing the intended purpose of the funds and transfer any savings therefrom to Trust Liabilities-DRRMF only when the project is completed.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 26. We recommend that management prioritize and provide adequate funding for the Municipal construction of the Landfill to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and monitor to ensure the protection of public health.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. Project had been completed.
observations.reason: Construction completed. However, reclassification of the Construction in Progress asset is still being adjusted.

observations.recommendation:
28. We recommend that ICO-Municipal Treasurer to restitute missing funds or submit immediately the liquidations of his cash advances, without prejudice to the filing of appropriate administrative and legal charges, if evidences warrant.
29. We also recommend the Municipal Mayor to initiate necessary steps for the immediate recovery of the missing funds from the former Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: In process. Case was filed.
observations.reason: No action taken to restitute the missing fund. The case was already filed.

observations.recommendation: 31. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer be required to submit the required Consolidated Financial Statements and CSACB for CYs 2010-2016, and henceforth to strictly comply with the provisions of the Accounting Systems Manual for Barangays.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer were required to submit the Consolidated Financial Statements and CSACB for CYs 2010-2016.

observations.recommendation:
37. retrieve the complete record of the financial assistance released to beneficiaries to reconcile with the balances recorded in the books of accounts;
38. require the Municipal Accountant to demand for the immediate liquidation of the financial assistance from all the beneficiaries in compliance with the terms and conditions in the Memorandum of Agreement in order to avoid the accumulation of unliquidated financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: Demand has been made during CY 2020 and 2021 for those who have not yet liquidated.

observations.recommendation: 39. We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the 736 pieces of Paid Disbursement Vouchers and its complete supporting documents to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: A memo was issued to present, only 5 DVs were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Only 5 DVs were submitted; others remain outstanding (Ref. Part III, CY 2015 AAR).

observations.recommendation: 43. We recommend that Management should ascertain the actual existence of the asset and if the same cannot be ascertained, verify the transactions that actually transpired and how the animals were disposed.
observations.managementAction: A memo was issued. Report on the conduct of physical count not furnished.
observations.reason: Report on the conduct of physical count was not provided.

observations.recommendation: 45. We recommend that Management strictly implement the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002, 96-004 and 2012-004 and require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to officials and employees with unsettled accounts and refrain from granting additional cash advances to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to protect the interest of the government. Further, suspend the salaries of officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances and submit justification/explanation why the persons responsible cannot be held liable on the reported loss of liquidation documents considering it was not submitted within the reglementary period, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension / Disallowance, if warranted by evidence.
observations.managementAction: Demand has been made during CY 2020 and 2021 for those who have not yet liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
22. maximize the utilization of appropriated funds in implementing the DRRM Fund response programs to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities.
23. categorize the identified PPAs into four (4) thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management to ensure compliance to item 3.0 of NEDA-DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001.
observations.managementAction: PPAs were not implemented due to COVID-19 but LDRRMIP submitted.
observations.reason: PPAs were categorized and LDRRMIP submitted; however, some PPAs were not implemented due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: 26. expedite construction of sanitary landfill and hasten closure of open dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. Project had been completed.
observations.reason: Construction completed; reclassification pending.

observations.recommendation: 28. restitute missing funds or submit liquidations; 29. Municipal Mayor to initiate recovery steps.
observations.managementAction: No action to restitute; case filed.
observations.reason: Case filed; restitution not yet effected.

observations.recommendation: 31. Submit required consolidated statements and CSACB for CYs 2010-2016 and comply with Accounting Systems Manual for Barangays.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer were required to submit the Consolidated Financial Statements and CSACB for CYs 2010-2016.

observations.recommendation: 37. retrieve complete records and 38. demand liquidation from beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Demand has been made during CY 2020 and 2021 for those who have not yet liquidated.

observations.recommendation: 39. submit 736 paid DVs and complete supporting documents to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Memo issued; only 5 DVs submitted.
observations.reason: Only partial submission achieved.

observations.recommendation: 43. ascertain actual existence of animals or verify transactions and disposition.
observations.managementAction: A memo was issued; report on physical count not furnished.
observations.reason: Report not submitted.

observations.recommendation: 45. implement Section 89 PD 1445 and COA Circulars; demand liquidation; suspend salaries where necessary.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters issued during CY 2020-2021.

observations.recommendation:
1.11. We recommended that:
a) The Accounting Office to draw a journal entry voucher (JEV) to
set-up an allowance for depreciation for the Road Network; Parks,
Plaza and Monuments; and Disaster Risk Reduction Equipment
accounts; and
b) The officers concerned strictly observe the accounting and
reporting requirements for the local road network construction
and maintenance syste as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-
008 dated November 23, 2015.

1.12. No Notice of Suspension or Disallowance was issued because the present
condition pertains to reporting and accounting deficiency.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, issue demand
letters to debtors to enforce collections or liquidation and
institute/file appropriate charges to defaulting debtors, for
unjustifiable failure to settle the account; and
2. Draw JEV to set-up an allowance for impairment of Due from
LGU and of Other Receivables.

2.10 No Notice of Suspension or Disallowance was issued because the present
condition pertains to reporting and accounting deficiency.

3.6 We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant be required to analyze the reciprocal accounts to determine the
causes of the discrepancies and adjust the books accordingly; henceforth, to
periodically reconcile the reciprocal accounts.

3.7 No Notice of Suspension or Disallowance was issued because the present
condition pertains to reporting and accounting deficiency.

4.11 We recommended that management expedite the implementation of
current and prior years development projects so that more desired benefits
could be enjoyed by the constituents as early as possible. Likewise, they
should review unimplemented PPAs in CY 2019 and priror years to ascertain
viability of their implementation for the ensuing year, otherwise, reprogram
for a more viable and significant PPAs in accordance to the socio-economic
development and environmental management requirements.

4.12 The Administration agrees with the audit recommendation and ensure
implementation and optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund and also
commits to implement the approved PPAs on or before the end of the Calendar
Year 2020. And to ensure compliance therewith, they will assign personnel from
the Office of the Mayor who will consciously monitor the utilization.

4.13 The deficiencies noted are mere administrative lapses in the faithful
compliance of regulation and there is no contingent loss on the part of the LGU
hence, does not necessitate the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

5.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure optimal
utilization and implementation of the Programs, Projects and Activities
(PPAs) supported by the LDRRM Fund under Capital Outlay. Likewise,
require the LDRRM Officer to include in its LDRRM Fund Investment
(LDRRMFIP) under a separate caption, the list of PPAs to be charged to the
unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund of previous years that was transferred
to the TF as required in Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

5.9 The deficiencies noted are mere administrative lapses in the faithful
compliance of regulation and there is no contingent loss on the part of the LGU
hence, does not necessitate the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

6.15 We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist maintain the
required record book for proper monitoring and to facilitate inventory
taking of agricultural implements received from DA-RFO8 and those
procured by the Municipality. We further recommended, that the
implementation of the program extended to the identified farmers
association in Pastrana, Leyte be covered by a Memorandum of Agreement
where conditions and responsibilities will be set out clearly.

6.16 The deficiencies noted are mere administrative lapses in the faithful
compliance of regulation and there is no contingent loss on the part of the LGU
hence, does not necessitate the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

7.5 We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal
Treasurers Office be required to initiate the quarterly release of the RPT
shares for all the barangays of the Municipality pursuant to Section 271(d) of
R.A. 7160.

7.6 The deficiencies noted are mere administrative lapses in the faithful
compliance of regulation and there is no contingent loss on the part of the LGU
hence, does not necessitate the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

8.7. We recommended that the Custodian or the Designated Inspection
Committee of LGU conduct an inspection every time accountable forms
are received from the Leyte Provincial Office and prepare the Inspection
and Acceptance Report, as assurance from defects and damages. Likewise,
issuance to Accountable Officers must be supported with Requisition Issue
Slip for recording and monitoring purposes.

8.8. The deficiencies noted are mere administrative lapses in the faithful
compliance of regulation and there is no contingent loss on the part of the LGU
hence, does not necessitate the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

9.10. We recommended that the:
a) Revenue Collection Clerk turnover their collections daily to the
Liquidating Officer and by the latter to the Municipal Treasurer,
supported by duly accomplished Report of Collections and
Deposits;
b) RCC and Liquidating Officer maintain and update regularly the
prescribed Cash book;
c) Liquidating Officer prepares daily, the Report of Collections and
Deposit, summarizing all collections received from the Revenue
Collection Clerks; and
d) Liquidating Officer to account the unused accountable forms in
the custody of the RCC, daily.

9.11. The deficiencies noted are mere administrative lapses in the faithful
compliance of regulation and there is no contingent loss on the part of the LGU
hence, does not necessitate the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

10.5. We recommended that the room of the Municipal Treasurer be
enclosed and occupied exclusively by her, separated from other personnel
under the Treasury department and that entry should be limited to
authorized personnel only.

10.6. We also recommended the provision of vault to ensure safety of
government funds and in compliance with sound security control.

10.7. The deficiencies noted are mere administrative lapses in the faithful
compliance of regulation and there is no contingent loss on the part of the LGU
hence, does not necessitate the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

11.7 We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management
Board:
1. Review and update their Solid Waste Management Plan required
by EMB for the approval of said plan; and
2. Propose for a separate collection schedules and or separate trucks
or haulers for specific types of wastes, otherwise the truck must
have a suitable compartment for storing sorted waste in
compliance with Sections 22 and 24 of RA 9003 and its approved
10-year Solid Waste Management Plan.

11.8 The deficiencies noted are mere administrative lapses in the faithful
compliance of regulation and there is no contingent loss on the part of the LGU
hence, does not necessitate the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

13.11 We recommended that:
1. The Sanggunian formulate and pass a local GAD code ordinance
with the assistance of the LGU GAD Focal Point System (GFPS),
to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to
ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and
integrated in local development plans; and
2. The GFPS of the Municipality regularly monitor the
implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that GAD
PPAs are effectively and efficiently implemented to attain GAD
objective.

13.12 The deficiencies noted are mere administrative lapses in the faithful
compliance of regulation and there is no contingent loss on the part of the LGU
hence, does not necessitate the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

1.7 We recommended that management thru Sanguniang Bayan should update
regularly its Local Revenue Code pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160 in order to
increase the financial capability of the LGU in rendering basic mandated services.

2.7 We recommended that management submit at the beginning of the year a
list of on-going and to be implemented projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for the
current year, and inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices are already posted as required in COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

3.5 We recommended that the LGU maintain a separate bank account for
funds received from DA RFO 8 in compliance with Section B, Article II of the
signed Memorandum of Agreement and for effective monitoring and recording of
transactions.

4.6 We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that
management ensure implementation and optimal utilization of the 20% DF to help
achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of
the LGU pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1
dated February 22, 2017.

5.11 We recommended that the municipality should:
a. maximize the utilization of appropriated funds in implementing the
various programs for risk reduction and management in order to
enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities.
b. categorize the identified PPAs into four (4) thematic areas of disaster
risk reduction and management to ensure compliance to item 3.0 of
NEDA-DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular NO. 2013-001.
c. include prior years’ unutilized LDRRM Fund in the preparation of
LDRRMF Investment Plan as required in Section 5.1.2 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer ensure that payment for Honoraria are subjected with the
corresponding withholding taxes using the Revised Withholding Tax Table
under Revenue Regulation No. 11-2018 and remit the same to the BIR within the
prescribed period.

We recommend that the GSO-Designate be required to update the Property Cards and Municipal Accountant to maintain all the Property Ledger Cards, and reconcile their records with the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit a Report of Unreleased Check to the Municipal Accountant at year-end and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to affected accounts for fair financial statements presentation.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be required to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers enumerated in Annex G, together with its supporting documents and the cancelled check with number 3880378. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of accounts pursuant to Section 347 RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that Management submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team. Also, require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to pay only claims with complete and appropriate supporting documents as provided under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree 1445.

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat be required to submit the lacking procurement documents. Likewise, require them to strictly observe the timely submission of perfected contracts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001, dated February 12, 2009.

We recommend that management ensure implementation and optimal utilization of the 20% DF to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommend that the municipality maximize utilization of appropriated funds in implementing the various programs for risk reduction and management in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities. Likewise, prepare an LDRRMF Investment Plan as required in Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and management agreed that the proceeds of the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property accruing to Special Education Fund (SEF) be automatically released to the Local School Board, so that the latter can prepare the Local School Board Budget as required in Sections 99 and 100 (b) of RA 7160. The budget must be approved by the Local School Board (LSB), duly supported with programs, projects and activities that are aligned with Section 100(b) of RA 7160, and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017.

We recommend that management prioritize and provide adequate funding for the construction of the Sanitary Landfill to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health.

We recommend that management appropriate in the LGU’s Annual Budget in the ensuing year, 1% of its IRA for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of their Local Council for Protection of Children as required by RA 9344 and reiterated in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant-Designate prioritize the
preparation and submission of the monthly bank reconciliation statements in
compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and maintain General and subsidiary
ledgers for the Cash in Bank accounts, as well as the presentation for audit of the
updated General and subsidiary ledgers.

We recommend that the:
1. Municipal Accountant-Designate record depreciation for CY 2016
and keep property ledger cards and account for the difference
between the increase in PPE during the year and the recorded
acquisitions in the amount of P3,612,661.17.
2. Property Officer-Designate conduct actual physical inventory of all
PPE, properly classified, and submit the inventory reports to COA.

We recommend that the Acting Municipal Accountant submit immediately the
complete financial statements and transaction documents for CY 2016 and henceforth
submit them promptly/regularly.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance
upon post-audit of transaction documents which were submitted very late.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor relieve the ICO-Municipal
treasurer from his present duties and responsibilities as ICO-Municipal Treasurer
pending resolution of his cash shortage.

We also recommend that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer restitute the missing
funds or submit immediately the liquidations of his cash advances, without prejudice
to the filing of appropriate administrative and legal charges, if evidences warrant.

We recommend that ICO-Municipal Treasurer to immediately restitute the
missing funds or submit immediately the liquidations of his cash advances, without
prejudice to the filing of appropriate administrative and legal charges, if evidences
warrant.

We also recommend the Municipal Mayor to initiate necessary steps for the
immediate recovery of the missing funds from the former Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that the accountable officer maintain a record for his cash
advances to provide him data of the amount of his cash advances and the balance of
his cash and accountable forms and prepare the monthly Reports of Accountability
for Accountable Forms with and without money value.

We recommend that the incoming and outgoing Municipal Treasurers render an
accurate invoice and receipt of accountabilities and make a formal turnover of such
accountabilities to the incumbent Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to supervise
the RCC’s and require them to remit daily and intact to the Liquidating Officer their
collections.

We also recommend that the Liquidating Officer assist the MTO in monitoring of
collection remittances by the RCCs.

We recommend that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer deposit his collections
daily and intact.

We did not issue a Notice of Disallowance/Suspension because this finding pertains
to procedural lapses in the handling of collections.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Accountant-Designate adjust the books of accounts if
they have been prepared already, to bring the accounts to its correct
balances, maintain supplies ledger cards and use the perpetual
inventory system in recording these inventories;
b. If the General Ledger has not been prepared yet see to it that the
correct amount is recognized in the books upon preparation, based
on the corrected CkDJ;
c. The Supply Officer-Designate to prepare periodic inventory reports
and maintain stock cards.

We recommend that:
1. the Municipal Accountant account for the difference of P4,575,050.00
in the recorded check issued;
2. the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the monthly Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms for checks for CY 2016, and
henceforth prepare the report regularly to accompany the Report of
Checks Issued for submission to the Accountant and the COA
Auditor.

We did not issue an NS/ND because the finding is procedural lapses.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer
be required to submit the Consolidated Financial Statements and Consolidated
Status of Appropriations, Commitments and Balances (CSACB) for CYs 2010-2016,
and henceforth to strictly comply with the provisions of the Accounting Systems
Manual for Barangays.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant-Designate record the fund to
the account Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (2-04-
01-020), keep subsidiary ledgers, make proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements and regularly submit the required monthly reports in strict compliance
with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the ten (10) buildings owned by the LGU be insured
against damage which may be caused by fire, earthquake, storm or other fortuitous
event with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

We did not issue a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance because the observation does
not entail pecuniary loss.

We recommend that all disbursement vouchers and their supporting
documents be stamped “Paid” by the Municipal Treasurer in compliance with
Section 2 (Q) of COA Circular No. 92-389.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer submit:
a. a justification why the structures for the three (3) subprojects were
not fit for occupancy despite the POWs having been completely
accomplished; and
b. proof of approval/review by the DILG of the POWs of the three (3)
subprojects.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer submit:
a. an explanation why the POWs for the subprojects was not submitted
when it was a condition for the release of the funds: and
b. proof that the revised POWs were submitted, if any.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to prepare the
monthly Report of Disbursements in compliance with Section 3e, Article III of the
MOA.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/disallowance where
warranted.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer be required to submit a
justification for the variation in the above-cited subprojects and the
approved/reviewed Program of Works for the variation.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance
where necessary.
